resources.name: Basic Structure
resources.description: Basic batch file structure and commands
resources.notes: @echo off hides commands from display, pause waits for keypress

resources.name: Variables
resources.description: Create and use variables in batch files
resources.notes: Use quotes for values with spaces, /a for arithmetic, /p for user input

resources.name: Command Line Arguments
resources.description: Access and use command line parameters
resources.notes: %0 is script name, %1-%9 are parameters, %* is all parameters

resources.name: Conditional Statements
resources.description: If/else statements and conditions
examples: if exist file.txt echo File found
if not exist backup mkdir backup
if defined variable echo Variable is set
resources.notes: Use /i for case-insensitive comparison, errorlevel checks return codes

resources.name: Loops
resources.description: For loops and iteration in batch files
resources.notes: Use %% in batch files, % in command line. /l for numeric, /d for directories

resources.name: Functions and Labels
resources.description: Create reusable code blocks with labels and functions
resources.notes: Use call to execute functions and return, goto jumps without return

resources.name: File Operations
resources.description: Work with files and directories
resources.notes: xcopy is more powerful than copy, /y suppresses prompts

resources.name: String Manipulation
resources.description: Manipulate strings and text in batch files
resources.notes: ~start,length for substrings, :old=new for replacement

resources.name: Error Handling
resources.description: Handle errors and check command success
resources.notes: errorlevel 0 means success, higher numbers indicate errors

resources.name: Environment Variables
resources.description: Access and modify environment variables
examples: if defined TEMP echo Temp directory: %TEMP%
resources.notes: setx creates permanent variables, set creates session variables

resources.name: Input/Output Redirection
resources.description: Redirect command input and output
resources.notes: > overwrites, >> appends, 2> redirects errors, | pipes to another command

resources.name: Date and Time Operations
resources.description: Work with dates and times in batch files
examples: for /f "tokens=1-3 delims=/" %%a in ('%date%') do set day=%%a
resources.notes: Date/time format varies by locale, use string manipulation to standardize

resources.name: Process Management
resources.description: Start, stop, and manage processes
resources.notes: /wait waits for completion, /min starts minimized, timeout adds delays

resources.name: Registry Operations
resources.description: Read from and write to Windows Registry
examples: for /f "tokens=3" %%a in ('reg query "HKLM\..." /v Version') do set ver=%%a
resources.notes: Use reg command for registry operations, be careful with system keys

resources.name: Network Operations
resources.description: Network-related batch operations
examples: ping -n 1 google.com > nul && echo Online || echo Offline
resources.notes: net use for network drives, netsh for network configuration

resources.name: File Content Processing
resources.description: Process and analyze file contents
resources.notes: usebackq allows quoted filenames, tokens and delims parse structured data

resources.name: Advanced Techniques
resources.description: Advanced batch scripting techniques
resources.notes: Delayed expansion needed for variables modified in loops/blocks

resources.name: Scheduling and Automation
resources.description: Schedule and automate batch file execution
resources.notes: schtasks for Windows Task Scheduler, at command for simple scheduling

resources.name: Security Considerations
resources.description: Security best practices for batch files
examples: rem Use quotes for file paths
resources.notes: Always validate input, use quotes, avoid executing user input directly

resources.name: User Interaction
resources.description: Get input from users and display messages effectively
examples: choice /c YN /m "Do you want to continue?"
if errorlevel 2 echo No was chosen
if errorlevel 1 echo Yes was chosen
msg * "This is a message to all logged in users"
resources.notes: choice returns errorlevel based on selection, set /p reads single line input, msg sends system messages

resources.name: System Information
resources.description: Retrieve and display various system details
resources.notes: systeminfo provides detailed OS info, wmic queries Windows Management Instrumentation, driverquery lists installed drivers

resources.name: Networking Utilities
resources.description: Additional network commands and tools
resources.notes: netstat shows network connections, tracert and pathping diagnose network paths, route manages routing tables, arp shows ARP cache

resources.name: Service Management
resources.description: Manage Windows services
examples: sc query type= service state= all
sc create MyService binPath= "C:\path\to\executable.exe" DisplayName= "My Custom Service"
resources.notes: net start/stop for basic control, sc for more advanced service configuration (query, create, delete, etc.)
